description_long: This object is a toy or replica shotgun styled after a Nerf blaster, featuring a predominantly beige and tan color scheme with orange accents at the muzzle and trigger area. The body is rectangular and blocky, with a long barrel that has venting holes along the top and a visible orange tip. The stock is skeletal and angular, constructed from a lighter material, and the grip is ergonomically shaped in dark gray. The magazine is boxy and protrudes downward, marked with the 'NERF' logo, suggesting a detachable design. The overall construction appears to be primarily plastic, with some parts possibly reinforced for durability. The surface is matte with some slight texturing, and the object is lightweight and designed for manual operation.
description: A beige and tan Nerf-style shotgun with a long vented barrel, orange accents, a skeletal stock, and a boxy magazine. The design is blocky, lightweight, and primarily plastic.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full length of the shotgun, including the vented barrel, orange muzzle, ergonomic grip, boxy magazine, and skeletal stock.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the circular orange muzzle and the narrow profile of the barrel.
description_view_2: Opposite side view displaying the same features as view 0, with the 'NERF' logo on the magazine more visible.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the end of the skeletal stock and the narrow width of the shotgun.
attribute_barrel_configuration: single-barrel with venting holes along the top and an orange muzzle tip
attribute_trigger_guard_design: rounded and elongated trigger guard with a slight forward extension, enclosing the trigger area
